parts.label: Primary Structure (3U)
parts.spec: 3U CubeSat primary frame, Al 7075, PC/104 standoffs, deployable rail guide grooves (CDS spec)

requirements: Provide a robust frame for the 3U CubeSat
Support deployable solar panel rails
Ensure compatibility with PC/104 standard
Enable thermal dissipation
Provide service access for avionics boards and battery
Ensure structural integrity under launch loads
Maintain thermal cycling from -40 to +80°C
Ensure electrical connectivity and isolation
Support field upgrades

risk.desc: Thermal cycling may cause deformation due to material mismatch between Al 7075 and CFRP.
risk.mit: Use thermal analysis to optimize material selection and design.

risk.desc: High-gain antenna may interfere with CubeSat's structural integrity during deployment.
risk.mit: Conduct structural analysis to ensure antenna deployment does not compromise the structure.

risk.desc: Insufficient clearance between avionics boards and frame may cause overheating.
risk.mit: Increase clearance between avionics boards and frame to improve heat dissipation.

risk.desc: Potential for fastener loosening due to vibration and thermal cycling.
risk.mit: Use locking nuts and washers to prevent fastener loosening.

risk.desc: Risk of electrical interference between avionics modules and frame.
risk.mit: Implement shielding and isolation measures to prevent electrical interference.

Ensure PCB stack compatibility with PC/104 standard

generated_parts.label: Antenna Deployer x 2
generated_parts.spec: Tape-spring UHF/S-band antenna deployer, stowed flat, burn-wire release, latching tab

requirements: Design an antenna deployer for a 3U CubeSat that stows a tape-spring UHF/S-band antenna flat and deploys it via a burn-wire release mechanism.
Ensure the deployer can handle thermal cycling from -40 to +80°C and launch vibration up to 20g.
The deployer must interface with a radio board and RF connector for signal transmission.
Provide service access for maintenance and inspection.
